message:
feat(ffs-cli): mount background scrub config plumbing (bd-4h62v)

First plumbing slice for bd-4h62v (Wire ScrubDaemon into ffs mount
lifecycle). Adds the configuration surface that the actual daemon
spawning will consume in subsequent commits, while keeping mount
behavior unchanged at runtime — no daemon is started from this
commit alone.

New imports staged for the lifecycle wiring:
- ffs_repair::pipeline::{GroupConfig, ScrubDaemon, ScrubDaemonConfig,
  ScrubDaemonMetrics, ScrubWithRecovery}
- std::fs::OpenOptions (alias StdOpenOptions to avoid clashing with
  the existing OpenFs OpenOptions usage elsewhere in the file)
- std::thread::JoinHandle
- std::time::Duration

DEFAULT_MOUNT_BACKGROUND_SCRUB_INTERVAL_SECS = 5
The default cadence for the background scrub when enabled — short
enough to surface corruption quickly during normal operation, long
enough to avoid pinning a CPU on idle filesystems. Subject to
revision once we have empirical numbers.

MountBackgroundScrubConfig struct (Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq):
- enabled: bool — final resolved on/off state
- explicit: bool — true if the user passed --background-scrub or
  --no-background-scrub, used downstream to decide whether to log
  the resolution as a user choice or a default
- interval_secs: u64 — seconds between scrub passes
- ledger_path: Option<PathBuf> — optional path for the scrub
  evidence ledger

resolve(read_write, background_scrub, no_background_scrub,
        interval_secs, ledger_path) -> Result<Self>:
- Rejects --background-scrub + --no-background-scrub as mutually
  exclusive (bail!).
- Rejects --background-scrub-interval-secs and
  --background-scrub-ledger when --no-background-scrub is also
  passed (each requires background scrub to be enabled).
- Default policy: enabled = !read_write — read-only mounts get
  scrub by default (read-only scrub is the safer surface and the
  most common case for forensics-style mounts), read-write mounts
  stay off by default (preserves prior behavior).
- explicit = background_scrub || no_background_scrub.
- interval_secs falls back to DEFAULT when not specified.

MountCmdOptions: add `background_scrub: MountBackgroundScrubConfig`
field. Subsequent commits will populate this from clap flags and
pass it into the mount runtime.

bd-4h62v stays in_progress; the ScrubWithRecovery
.with_repair_writes_enabled gate landed in the prior commit
provides the read-only-safe defaults that this config will drive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
